content.problemStatement:
Chef and his girlfriend are going to have a promenade. They are walking along the straight road which consists of segments placed one by one. Before walking Chef and his girlfriend stay at the beginning of the first segment, they want to achieve the end of the last segment. 

There are few problems: 


 At the beginning Chef should choose constant integer - the velocity of moving. It can't be changed inside one segment. 
 The velocity should be decreased by at least 1 after achieving the end of some segment. 
 There is exactly one shop on each segment. Each shop has an attractiveness. If it's attractiveness is **W** and Chef and his girlfriend move with velocity **V** then if **V** < **W** girlfriend will run away into the shop and the promenade will become ruined. 
 Chef doesn't want to lose her girl in such a way, but he is an old one, so you should find the minimal possible velocity at the first segment to satisfy all conditions.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of segments, and the second line contains N space-separated integers W1, W2, ..., WN denoting the attractiveness of shops.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimal possible velocity at the beginning.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the minimum velocity is 6 because it is the maximum attractiveness of the shops. For the second test case, the minimum velocity is 3 because it is the maximum attractiveness of the shops.
The minimum velocity is 3 because it is the maximum attractiveness of the shops.
The minimum velocity is 10 because it is the maximum attractiveness of the shops.

solution.approach: The key insight is to start with the maximum attractiveness of the shops and then decrease the velocity by at least 1 after each segment. This ensures that the promenade is not ruined and the girlfriend does not run away into any shop. The algorithm works by iterating through the attractiveness values and updating the minimum velocity accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space complexity is constant as we only use a fixed amount of space to store the input and the minimum velocity.
